title: PMTemperature
description: Integrates popular temperature mods with ProtoManly's Weather
body:
# PMTemperature
This mod adds compatibility between temperature mods, including [Cold Sweat](https://modrinth.com/mod/cold-sweat) and/or [Tough as Nails](https://modrinth.com/mod/tough-as-nails), and [ProtoManly's Weather Mod](https://modrinth.com/mod/protomanlys-weather) by integrating their temperature systems together.

## Features
- Cold Sweat / TaN world temperatures will directly correspond with the temperature calculated by PMWeather
- Rain cooling has been localized according to PMWeather's precipitation system

### Apparent Temperature (optional)
- Simulates how the human body perceives and reacts to temperature in real life. _Apparent temperature_ describes the modified temperature supplied to Cold Sweat when this feature is active
- Wind chill will take effect for temperatures below 0°C. It depends on the local wind speed; for example, a passing squall in a snowy biome may lower the apparent temperature up to 10° below the world temperature
- For temperatures above 19°C, humidity will raise the apparent temperature. Jungles and other hot/humid biomes are most affected by this change

### Compatibility
- Cold Sweat and TaN will use their default climate systems in dimensions where PMWeather isn't enabled
- [Serene Seasons](https://modrinth.com/mod/serene-seasons) is still supported by proxy of PMWeather's own temperature system

## Issues & Support
Please report all issues either on GitHub or in the designated thread in the PMWeather discord server. **DO NOT** report temperature issues to ProtoManly, Glitchfiend or Mikul when this is installed. It's a waste of time for both you and the developers.


### FAQ

**Q: When will this be updated to [X]?**

A: Whenever both PMWeather and at least 1 temperature mod become available on that version.

**Q: How strong are the apparent temperature effects?**

A: The mod implements the wind chill and [Humidex](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Humidex) formulas used by Environment Canada. Try out [this calculator](https://weather.gc.ca/windchill/wind_chill_e.html) if you want to mess around with the values.
